name: Doorway with frame and lintel
description: A framed doorway punched into a thin wall section: a 2-tall door at the bottom-center with two accent jambs on either side, a 3-wide accent lintel above the head, and a stair-cap course that overhangs outward to shelter the entrance. Optionally hangs a lantern at head height for night arrivals.

required_furniture.rule: bottom-center on the wall axis, oriented to `facing`
directly above the lower half, same facing
one cell on each side of the door, 2 tall, in accent material
3-wide accent course directly above the door head, spanning jamb to jamb
stairs above the lintel, overhanging outward toward `facing`
optional, beside the door at head height; toggled via `with_lantern` kwarg

placement_rules: Door must be 2 tall with [half=lower] below [half=upper]
Stair cap faces opposite the door so the slope sheds outward
Wall axis is inferred from `facing` (north/south → wall runs along X; east/west → wall runs along Z)
lighting_ventilation: Doorway provides the primary daylight aperture of the room it serves; the optional lantern adds a warm night light at head height. If multiple doors land on the same wall, leave at least 2 blocks of wall between their frames.

medieval.notes: Heavy stone-brick surround with an oak door — reads as a keep or longhouse entry.

modern.notes: Flush smooth-stone jambs with an iron door; lantern often omitted in favor of redstone lamps elsewhere.

fantasy.notes: Purpur surround and dark oak door evoke an enchanted or temple entrance; lantern strongly recommended.

python_skill_signature: build(aabb: AABB, materials: Materials, style: str, *, facing: str = 'south', with_lantern: bool = True) -> list[Op]
